qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary command execution via configuration file backtick syntax
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes command injection via config file backtick syntax (glances/config.py:331-336, glances/globals.py:159-165). Advisory describes SQL injection in TimescaleDB export module via unsanitized process names (glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py:79-93 normalize() function). Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different attack vector.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST API exposes complete configuration file with secrets via /api/config endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes information disclosure via /api/config endpoint exposing secrets. Advisory describes SQL injection in TimescaleDB export module. Different vulnerable code, different vulnerability class, different root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST API exposes all system monitoring data without authentication by default
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes missing authentication on REST API. Advisory describes SQL injection in TimescaleDB export module. Different vulnerable code, different vulnerability class, different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes SQL injection in the TimescaleDB export module (glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py) where the normalize() function fails to escape single quotes in string values before concatenating them into SQL INSERT queries. None of the three qualifying findings identify this vulnerability. Finding 1 (command_injection_via_config) addresses a different vulnerability in config.py/globals.py. Finding 2 (config_exposure_via_api) addresses information disclosure via REST API. Finding 3 (no_auth_api_exposure) addresses missing authentication. No finding references the TimescaleDB export module, the normalize() function, SQL injection via process names, or the vulnerable code locations specified in the advisory (lines 79-93, 201-205 in glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py).
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-30930 specifies vulnerable code at glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py:79-93 (normalize function) and lines 201-205 (query construction)

Advisory root cause: normalize() function uses f"'{value}'" without escaping embedded single quotes, enabling SQL injection via process names, mount points, network interface names, or container names
Finding command_injection_via_config references glances/config.py:331-336 and glances/globals.py:159-165 (different vulnerable code)
Finding no_auth_api_exposure references glances/main.py:45-46, glances/outputs/glances_restful_api.py:258-272, glances/server.py:194-196 (different vulnerable code)
No finding mentions TimescaleDB, normalize(), SQL injection, process names, or the advisory-specified vulnerable functions
